oai_response.prompt:
Here is code in Python 3 programming lanaguge. Translate the following code from Python 3 to C programming lanaguge. Do not output any extra description or tokens other than the translated code. 

n1=int(input())
a=int(n1/1234567)
b=int(n1/123456)
t=0
resultado=0
for x in range(0,a+1):
    for i in range(0,b+1):
        resultado=n1-(1234567*x+123456*i)
        if(resultado%1234==0 and resultado>=0):
            print("YES")
            t=1
            break
    if(t==1):
        break
if(t==0):
    print("NO")
||END-of-SRC||

source_data.prob_desc_notes: NoteIn the first sample, one of the possible solutions is to buy one house, one car and one computer, spending 1 234 567 + 123 456 + 1234 = 1 359 257 game-coins in total.
source_data.prob_desc_input_spec: The first line of the input contains a single integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 109) — Kolya's initial game-coin score.
source_data.prob_desc_output_spec: Print "YES" (without quotes) if it's possible that Kolya spent all of his initial n coins buying only houses, cars and computers. Otherwise print "NO" (without quotes).
source_data.prob_desc_description: Kolya is developing an economy simulator game. His most favourite part of the development process is in-game testing. Once he was entertained by the testing so much, that he found out his game-coin score become equal to 0.Kolya remembers that at the beginning of the game his game-coin score was equal to n and that he have bought only some houses (for 1 234 567 game-coins each), cars (for 123 456 game-coins each) and computers (for 1 234 game-coins each).Kolya is now interested, whether he could have spent all of his initial n game-coins buying only houses, cars and computers or there is a bug in the game. Formally, is there a triple of non-negative integers a, b and c such that a × 1 234 567 + b × 123 456 + c × 1 234 = n?Please help Kolya answer this question.
